item.title: Musk’s Trial Against OpenAI Hits Some Rough Spots in First Week
item.description: Elon Musk set out to tell a jury that his falling out with OpenAI was a simple tale of betrayal.

item.title: Columbia Risks Losing Top Credit Rating as Trump Slams Colleges
item.description: Columbia University’s credit outlook was revised to negative by Moody’s Ratings, which cited rising risks tied to the “federal environment for higher education.”

item.title: EBay Soars on Report That GameStop Is Preparing a Takeover Bid
item.description: EBay Inc. jumped more than 13% in after-hours trading on Friday after the Wall Street Journal reported that video-game retailer GameStop Corp. is preparing a bid for the company.

item.title: Archroma Reaches Final Terms on Much Delayed Loan Extension
item.description: Specialty chemicals producer Archroma sweetened the terms on a junk loan deal to extend around $1 billion of debt, inching closer to completing a transaction that has faced multiple delays over the past month.

item.title: Pershing Square IPO Investors Post Gain After Free Shares
item.description: Investors in Bill Ackman’s new $5 billion closed-end fund ended the week with a small gain overall, once the value of the free shares they received in his asset management company is included.

item.title: LA’s Television City Studio Lot Faces Sale Forced by Lenders
item.description: Lenders are forcing Hollywood landlord Hackman Capital Partners, the largest independent studio and soundstage owner, to relinquish entertainment properties around Los Angeles as values plunge and demand stays soft.

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi to Revamp Indo-Pacific Vision in Vietnam Speech
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi on Saturday will use a speech in Vietnam to lay out her vision for the Indo-Pacific, as she seeks to bolster economic and security ties across the region in a move that could counter an increasingly assertive China.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras Is Said to Target Up to $4 Billion in IPO
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $4 billion in its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, as demand for the artificial intelligence chipmaker and data center operator’s shares heats up.

item.title: Retailer West Marine Readies Possible Bankruptcy to Close Stores
item.description: Boat retailer West Marine Inc. is laying the groundwork for a potential Chapter 11 bankruptcy to restructure its debt, including leases, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Apple Raises Mac Mini’s Starting Price to $799 After AI Frenzy Drains Supply
item.description: Apple Inc. raised the starting price of its Mac mini desktop to $799 from $599, adjusting to inventory shortages driven by AI demand and the tight supply of processors.

item.title: Disney’s New CEO Is Exploring a ‘Super App’ For Theme Park Tickets, Movies and More
item.description: Walt Disney Co. senior executives are discussing how to unify the company’s disparate mobile apps and turn its streaming service into the first stop for all things Disney, a place where users can book park tickets, buy merchandise, play games and watch movies, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Fast-Money Hedge Fund Trades Roar as Wall Street Defies Gloom
item.description: Wall Street is back in triumphant mode, with stocks at records, risk appetites refreshed — and alternative strategies that run on their own engine winning, too.

item.title: OpenAI’s Revenue Chief Says Enterprise Business ‘Accelerating’
item.description: A recent report raised concerns about OpenAI missing its growth targets.

item.title: The Online Games Where the House Always Wins
item.description: Online casino-style games like Monopoly GO! are huge. Could new scrutiny challenge how they do business?

item.title: Retail Traders Burnt on Both Sides of April’s Chip Rip: ETF IQ
item.description: SOXL and SOXS saw billions of dollars in wrong-way bets in April

item.title: Big Oil CEOs Warn Energy Is Moving Closer to Cliff’s Edge
item.description: America’s biggest oil companies are warning that global crude markets may be nearing an inflection point of higher prices the longer the Strait of Hormuz stays closed.

item.title: BMO Turns to AI and Quantum Computing to Predict Earthquakes
item.description: Bank of Montreal is betting on artificial intelligence and quantum computing to help predict earthquakes and respond to wildfires as part of the Canadian bank’s expansion in the US.

item.title: Brown University Slashed Stake in Blue Owl Private Credit Fund
item.description: Brown University’s $8 billion endowment cut its position in a Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit fund by more than half last quarter, joining retail investors in scaling back exposure to the $1.8 trillion market.

item.title: Venezuela Oil Exports Hit 1 Million Barrels a Day in Post-Maduro Era
item.description: Venezuela’s crude exports rose for a third month, surpassing 1 million barrels a day in April and highlighting a swift rebound less than six months after the ouster of strongman Nicolas Maduro.

item.title: Fed’s Bowman Says Mythos Shows ‘Dynamic Nature’ of AI Tools
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s top bank cop said regulators must consider how best to supervise new technology like Anthropic PBC’s Mythos, as officials grapple with the potential risks of artificial intelligence models.

item.title: Bond Traders Hedge for Both Cuts and Hikes After Fed Division
item.description: A rare opportunity is opening up in rates derivatives underlying the $31 trillion Treasury market: Division at the Federal Reserve has traders placing bets on interest-rate cuts next year while also hedging for a hike.

item.title: Congo Ex-President Kabila Denounces US Sanctions Against Him
item.description: Former president of the Democratic Republic of Congo, Joseph Kabila, denounced US sanctions against him for his alleged ties to a rebel group occupying a large part of the country’s mineral-rich east.

item.title: Mercuria, Heeney Ink Venezuelan Mining Deals for Gold, Metals
item.description: Mercuria Energy Group has secured several agreements to purchase Venezuelan bulk commodities and metals, including gold, in the latest example of trading houses brokering investment deals in the country after former leader Nicolas Maduro’s ouster.

item.title: EU Parliament Trade Chief Calls Trump Tariff Threat Unacceptable
item.description: The head of the European Parliament’s trade committee called President Donald Trump’s threat to boost tariffs on EU cars and trucks “unacceptable,” and criticized the US for being an unreliable partner.

item.title: Exxon CEO Feels ‘Positive’ on Venezuela After Earlier Criticism
item.description: Exxon Mobil Corp. is looking seriously at reinvesting in Venezuela just months after Chief Executive Officer Darren Woods told President Donald Trump the South American nation’s oil sector was “uninvestable.”

item.title: War Rebound Sends Muni Bonds to Best April Since 2014
item.description: The municipal-bond market roared back in April to deliver its best performance for that month in over a decade, as investors found their footing following a stretch of war-fueled volatility.

item.title: Nuclear AI Startup Fermi Promised Land and Ample Power. But It Couldn't Sign a Single Client
item.description: Now the ex-CEO is waging a battle for Fermi’s future and its vision of atomic-powered data centers in the Texas panhandle.

item.title: ‘Final Fantasy XIV’ Faces Key Test as Thousands of Fans Gather
item.description: The longrunning online game has a lot of work to do to recover from the disappointing ‘Dawntrail’ expansion

item.title: Meta Acquires Robotics AI Company to Help Build Humanoid Technology
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. has acquired Assured Robot Intelligence, a startup developing artificial intelligence models for robots, as part of a major initiative to build humanoid technology.

item.title: Surprise Colombian Rate Decision Helps Avoid Volatility, Central Banker Says
item.description: Colombia’s surprise decision to halt interest rate increases this week was intended to prevent upheaval, but may come at a cost, according to central bank co-director Mauricio Villamizar.
